posts.text: I built a Threads account that writes, posts, and improves without me touching it. It uses the official Meta API. Free tools. One-time setup. And yes, you’re reading a post it wrote right now.

3 things nobody tells you about hands-off Threads growth:

1. The official Threads API exists and is free — no shady bots, no browser hacks.
2. A single 2-hour setup with GitHub Actions + free AI tools posts forever. Zero monthly cost.
3. The system reads your own analytics daily and writes better posts tomorrow. It's a loop that compounds.
4. (Bonus) Token refresh? Also automated. You literally never log in again.

Full setup guide in my bio.

Most creators waste hours manually posting every day. Instead, build a feedback loop:  
Post → Read analytics → Let AI rewrite tomorrow’s content based on what worked.  

I set this up once with free tools (GitHub Actions + free AI APIs). It now runs itself. No daily grind.  

That’s the whole trick. One setup, zero upkeep.

Waking up to 7 draft posts written from yesterday’s best performers.  
No alarm for “content time.” No panic about what to say.  

Before: 2 hours a day guessing, burning out, going silent on weekends.  
After: 2 hours one Saturday, then it runs itself. Writes, posts, studies analytics, gets smarter.  

Same account. Same audience. One decision changed everything.

Before automation:

4 things nobody tells you about hands-free Threads growth:

1. The official Meta API is the difference between “automation that works” and “automation that gets banned.”
2. Free tools (GitHub Actions + free AI APIs) run everything — no SaaS bill, no subscription creep.
3. The real magic isn’t posting. It’s the daily analytics loop: read what worked, write better tomorrow.
4. Tokens auto-refresh. You set it up once and genuinely never log in again.

I haven't touched this account in 3 months. It still posts every day. Gets better every week.

The secret isn't willpower. It's one afternoon of setup using free tools and the official Meta API. The system writes, posts, reads analytics, and rewrites tomorrow's posts — all on its own. Even refreshes its own tokens.

Consistency without the burnout. That's the whole game.

The "post every day or die" advice is keeping you small. Consistency matters — but showing up to manually type posts every single morning is not a strategy. It's a tax on your time. The real win? Build a system that posts for you, learns from itself, and never burns out. That's what runs this account. One 2-hour setup. Free tools. Official Meta API. Then it compounds on its own while you build something real.

Now my account posts every day without me. It reads yesterday’s analytics and writes better tomorrow. Gets smarter while I sleep. I haven’t touched the app in weeks.
